stories.title: Are we human? Why ByteDance and Alibaba are disabling AI agents in China
stories.simple_summary: Large Chinese companies ByteDance and Alibaba are turning off AI agents (programs that make decisions and take actions on their own) in China. This appears to be a response to rules from their government.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to people in China and to tech companies — not something that affects your daily life or small business unless you operate in China.

stories.title: New Google commercial imagines a Declaration of Independence written with help from AI
stories.simple_summary: Google made an advertisement showing what the Declaration of Independence might have looked like if the Founding Fathers had used Google's AI writing tools to help write it.
stories.why_it_matters: This is just a creative advertisement — it doesn't affect you, but it shows how companies are trying to make AI tools seem helpful for important writing tasks.

stories.title: The Artificial Intelligence (AI) Memory Supercycle Is Getting Stronger. Here's How You Can Profit From This Boom With Less Than $100
stories.simple_summary: Someone is promoting a way to invest money in AI computer memory companies, claiming you can profit from it with small amounts of money.
stories.why_it_matters: This is investment advice for people with money to invest — nothing you need to act on unless you're looking to buy stocks.

stories.title: AI security questions loom over NATO summit
stories.simple_summary: Military and government leaders are discussing safety concerns about AI at a NATO meeting.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly relevant to government officials and military planners — not something that affects your business or daily life right now.

stories.title: Midjourney wants Hollywood studios to reveal the details of their AI usage
stories.simple_summary: A company called Midjourney (which makes AI image software) is asking Hollywood movie studios to explain how they use AI in their own work, as part of a legal dispute.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and entertainment lawyers — nothing you need to worry about.

stories.title: Alibaba reportedly bans employees from using Claude Code
stories.simple_summary: The company Alibaba told its employees they cannot use a specific AI tool called Claude Code because they consider it risky or unsafe.
stories.why_it_matters: This mainly matters to Alibaba's employees — it shows companies are being cautious about which AI tools they allow, but it doesn't affect your small business.

stories.title: What is Mistral AI? Everything to know about the OpenAI competitor
stories.simple_summary: Mistral AI is a newer AI company (similar to OpenAI which makes ChatGPT) that has raised a lot of money and wants to make powerful AI tools available to everyone.
stories.why_it_matters: This gives you another AI option if you want to try AI tools, though ChatGPT and Google's AI are still more well-known and widely used.

stories.title: The Impact of Encounters with Alien Intelligence on the Future of Humanity
stories.simple_summary: An article discusses what might happen if humans ever encountered intelligent life from outer space.
stories.why_it_matters: This is science fiction thinking — not related to AI technology you use or need to understand.

stories.title: The fanfiction community is at war with AI — and itself
stories.simple_summary: Writers in the fanfiction community (people who write stories based on existing books and shows) are fighting with each other about whether using AI to write stories is acceptable, and trying to catch who's using AI.
stories.why_it_matters: Unless you write fanfiction, this doesn't affect you — but it shows creative communities are struggling with questions about AI and human work.

stories.title: Silicon Valley faith-based co-working spaces emerging as AI prompts reflection on changing industry
stories.simple_summary: Some companies in Silicon Valley (tech industry area) are opening shared office spaces focused on faith and spirituality, partly because people are thinking more deeply about AI and its impact on society.
stories.why_it_matters: This doesn't affect you — it's mainly about what tech workers are thinking about.

stories.title: The only AI glossary you’ll need this year
stories.simple_summary: Someone created a list explaining all the new AI words and terms people are using, to help people understand the vocabulary.
stories.why_it_matters: This could actually be helpful if you want to understand AI words you hear — it's a resource guide, not news about a change.

stories.title: The browser wars aren’t about search anymore — here are the best alternatives to Chrome and Safari
stories.simple_summary: An article reviews web browsers (like Chrome and Safari that you use to look at websites) and lists alternatives you could try instead.
stories.why_it_matters: This is about web browsers, not AI — it has nothing to do with artificial intelligence.
